title: CMDB Accuracy Scoring
spl:
index=cmdb sourcetype="cmdb:validation" earliest=-7d
| eval ok=if(match(lower(to_string(check_passed)),"(?i)true|1|pass|ok"),1,0)
| stats avg(ok) as accuracy_ratio by ci_class
| eval accuracy_pct=round(accuracy_ratio*100,1)
| sort accuracy_pct
description: Scores accuracy by sampling validation (discovery match, pingable, owner confirmed) ‚Äî complements completeness-focused quality scores.
value: Scores accuracy by sampling validation (discovery match, pingable, owner confirmed) ‚Äî complements completeness-focused quality scores.
implementation: Ingest periodic validation (e.g., ‚ÄúIP matches DNS,‚Äù ‚Äúserver responds to agent,‚Äù ‚Äúowner replied‚Äù). Aggregate pass rate per class and region. Trend monthly for governance scorecards.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Install and configure the required add-on or app: `Splunk_TA_snow` + discovery/network.
‚Ä¢ Ensure the following data sources are available: `snow:cmdb_ci`, validation job results (`cmdb:validation`).
‚Ä¢ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
Ingest periodic validation (e.g., ‚ÄúIP matches DNS,‚Äù ‚Äúserver responds to agent,‚Äù ‚Äúowner replied‚Äù). Aggregate pass rate per class and region. Trend monthly for governance scorecards.

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=cmdb sourcetype="cmdb:validation" earliest=-7d
| eval ok=if(match(lower(to_string(check_passed)),"(?i)true|1|pass|ok"),1,0)
| stats avg(ok) as accuracy_ratio by ci_class
| eval accuracy_pct=round(accuracy_ratio*100,1)
| sort accuracy_pct
```

Understanding this SPL

**CMDB Accuracy Scoring** ‚Äî Scores accuracy by sampling validation (discovery match, pingable, owner confirmed) ‚Äî complements completeness-focused quality scores.

Documented **Data sources**: `snow:cmdb_ci`, validation job results (`cmdb:validation`). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `Splunk_TA_snow` + discovery/network. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: cmdb; **sourcetype**: cmdb:validation. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Scopes the data: index=cmdb, sourcetype="cmdb:validation", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
‚Ä¢ `eval` defines or adjusts **ok** ‚Äî often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
‚Ä¢ `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by ci_class** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
‚Ä¢ `eval` defines or adjusts **accuracy_pct** ‚Äî often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
‚Ä¢ Orders rows with `sort` ‚Äî combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Bar chart (accuracy % by class), Gauge (fleet accuracy), Line chart (trend).
visualization: Bar chart (accuracy % by class), Gauge (fleet accuracy), Line chart (trend).
